design_philosophy.core_loop: Guide autonomous lemmings through themed levels by assigning limited skill uses to create safe passages. Players pause, assign skills to individuals, and observe outcomes. Multiple valid solutions exist for each level.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (limited skill uses per level) vs. lemming preservation (need to save enough lemmings to proceed through world progression). Tension between trial-and-error learning and consequence management.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a wise guide/shepherd directing small creatures toward safety. The fantasy emphasizes agency through puzzle-solving rather than direct control—you orchestrate, not command.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Lemmings 2 is opinionated about failure being a learning tool, not punishment. No lives lost permanently—players can 'nuke' levels to restart at no cost. The design prioritizes puzzle exploration over execution skill, allowing players to experiment freely. The twelve tribes structure allows players to avoid stuck levels entirely rather than forcing grinding. Expansion from 8 to 43+ skills represents a philosophy shift toward mechanical variety and tribe identity over minimalism.

difficulty.lives_system: No lives system. Lemmings lost in a level persist into the next level of that world, creating cascading consequence (earning gold requires 50+ survivors). Players can 'nuke' all lemmings to reset a level without penalty.
difficulty.checkpointing: World-based structure with ten levels per world. Progress saves between worlds. Levels can be replayed within a world to earn more lemming survivors for subsequent levels.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Very high. Players can tackle twelve worlds in any order (non-linear progression). If stuck on a level, they can attempt other worlds instead. Medal system (gold/silver/bronze) provides granular difficulty scaling without hard gates—only one lemming reaching exit needed to progress, but gold medals require conservation strategies.

progression.upgrade_system: Skill availability system: 43+ skills distributed across tribes with varying frequency per world. Skills are temporary consumables (limited uses per level), not permanent upgrades. Classic tribe preserves original game mechanics as a nostalgic variant.
progression.unlock_mechanism: World selection is open after initial access. Twelve talisman pieces (one per world) must be collected by achieving gold medals on all ten levels of each world to complete the game. No hard progression gates—players choose order of engagement.
replayability_hooks: Medal rankings incentivize replaying levels with conservation strategies (gold requires ~50+ survivors, silver/bronze have looser requirements)
Twelve worlds with distinct aesthetic tribes and skill distributions encourage multiple playstyles
Practice mode allows experimenting with any eight skills in custom configurations
Fast-forward feature enables speedrun-style engagement after mastery
Lemming pool carries over within world, incentivizing strategic replay to accumulate survivors

world_building_techniques: Tribal theming (Highland, Cavelem, Circus, etc.) with visual/cultural variations in lemming appearance
Narrative framing: ancient prophecy of darkness on Lemming Island, lemmings must collect twelve talisman pieces to escape
World names and aesthetic design convey tribe identity without explicit narrative exposition
Plot serves as structure justification rather than moment-to-moment storytelling

faction_indicators: Twelve distinct tribes with individual identities (Highland, Cavelem, Circus, etc.)
Classic tribe as nostalgic faction preserving original game mechanics
Tribe selection potentially signals player preference for specific skill sets and aesthetics
danger_signals: No permanent failure states reduce social friction—players cannot permanently lock themselves out
Nuke mechanic allows graceful exit from no-win situations without shame
Medal system creates no competitive hierarchy that divides players into skill tiers
environmental_storytelling: Level obstacles and hazards reflect tribal aesthetics and themes
Skill availability per world subtly teaches mechanical vocabulary through tribe-specific challenges
Cumulative lemming loss across world levels tells implicit resource management narrative

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Lemmings 2: The Tribes SNES)
